capability_statement: Operators can inspect provider incident recovery before advancing a provider-backed action.
before_state: AOF could show a controlled provider execution candidate, but failure handling was mostly boundary text.
after_state: AOF records detection, containment, rollback decision, recovery action, operator notification, learning update, and resume/stop governance as auditable recovery evidence.
scenario: A GitHub issue creation candidate receives an unexpected provider response; AOF stops calls, preserves evidence, checks rollback readiness, notifies the operator, and defers resume until governance review.
five_minute_demo: Run provider-incident-recovery-audit and release-state-audit from a clean checkout.
time_saved_or_work_reduced: Operator no longer manually reconstructs failure handling from candidate, boundary, rollback, outcome, and learning artifacts.
cognitive_load_removed: Operator can distinguish controlled candidate, incident drill, and production recovery performance without reading every raw JSON artifact.

capability_matrix.before: Incident handling was mostly boundary text attached to provider execution readiness.
capability_matrix.after: Incident handling is a dedicated drill with detection, containment, rollback, recovery, notification, learning, and resume/stop governance.
capability_matrix.user_value: Operators can inspect failure handling before advancing provider execution.

not_proven: This value evidence does not prove live incident recovery performance, actual rollback execution, third-party adoption, or market value.
